svg.text: Same receptor, two activation modes → divergent signaling
HTNV binds β3 integrin ~3.6 kcal/mol more strongly
HTNV preferentially stabilizes extended-open β3 integrin (p < 0.001)
HDX-MS: β3 integrin deuterium exchange (15 min)
HTNV Gc induces greater protection in PSI/EGF domains (p < 0.01)

Fig. 2 | Atomic-scale divergence of Gc–β3 integrin interaction. A, Sequence alignment highlighting 15-aa divergence region. B, Structural schematic of Gc–β3 complex. C, Binding free energy (MM-GBSA). D, Per-residue energy decomposition. E, β3 integrin conformational populations from 500 ns MD. F, HDX-MS protection map validating MD predictions.
